title: The Small Reading Club
contentEn: Mina and her friends started a reading club in the school library. They did not choose long novels at first. Instead, they picked short stories with clear topics and useful words.

Every Friday, each student shared one sentence that they liked. Then the group discussed why the sentence worked well. Sometimes they noticed a new phrase, and sometimes they found a better way to say a simple idea.
After two months, Mina felt a real change. She still met difficult words, but she was no longer afraid of them. She learned to use context, examples, and repeated reading to understand more.
contentZh: 米娜和朋友们在学校图书馆成立了一个阅读俱乐部。她们一开始没有选择长篇小说，而是挑选主题清晰、词汇实用的短篇故事。
每周五，每位学生都会分享一句自己喜欢的句子。然后小组讨论这句话为什么写得好。有时她们会注意到一个新短语，有时会发现表达简单想法的更好方式。
两个月后，米娜感受到了真实的变化。她仍然会遇到难词，但已经不再害怕。她学会了借助上下文、例子和重复阅读来理解更多内容。

questions.prompt: Why did the club choose short stories first?
questions.promptZh: 为什么这个俱乐部一开始选择短篇故事？

options.label: They were easier to discuss and understand.
Their teacher ordered them to do so.

questions.explanation: 第一段强调短篇故事主题清晰、词汇实用，因此更容易理解和讨论。

questions.prompt: What helped Mina feel less afraid of difficult words?
questions.promptZh: 什么帮助米娜不再害怕难词？

options.label: She learned to use context and examples.
She translated every word into Chinese.

questions.explanation: 最后一段明确提到 context、examples 和 repeated reading。

questions.prompt: The club started with long novels because Mina liked difficult books.
questions.promptZh: 这个俱乐部一开始读长篇小说，因为米娜喜欢难书。
questions.explanation: 第一段说明她们一开始没有选择长篇小说。
